_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.10 NCL Encore Pacific Coast Solo Group pack. Built fresh 2026-06-11 during a careful-not-clever pass + content boardroom. Primary-source verification: Wikipedia (Norwegian Encore), Wikipedia (Lions Gate Bridge), The Points Guy + Cruise Critic + CruiseMapper for Encore Speedway specifications and Manhattan Room deck location, NCL.com for service-related figures. Three-lens content boardroom (Documentary Observational / Gemini 2.5 Flash; Travel Writer The Second / GPT-4o; Editor-in-Chief / Perplexity) ran blind in vendor-diverse rounds 2026-06-11; consensus-high adopted content surfaced in pack body. Verdict file at .private/boardroom/encore-content-verdict-2026-06-11.md (operator-private per boardroom hard rule #5).
verification_session: Sailing is October 12-18, 2026 (123 days out). Full read of all 748 pre-fix lines, primary-source verification of every suspect claim, factual corrections, voice pass, content boardroom (triad x 3 vendors blind), adopted-content execution. Pack at 813 lines post-edits.

length_ft_m.value: 1,094 ft / 333 m (Wikipedia cites 333.44 m / 1,094.0 ft; pack matches)

crew.notes: Pack pre-edit said '~2,100 crew' which matches Wikipedia exactly. NOT the training-data carryover pattern that affected Prima/Aqua/Luna drafts ('~1,388'). No correction needed.

year_built.value: Delivered October 30, 2019 by Meyer Werft at Papenburg, Germany

godparent.notes: godparent key added 2026-07-06 for the current gate schema; identical verified fact to christening.godmother (Kelly Clarkson).

route.notes: Pack pre-edit said 'transatlantic from Southampton' which is accurate but did not specify NY destination; left as-is since 'transatlantic from Southampton' is sufficient and correct.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: $20/day per person at most cabin tiers; $25/day in Suites/Haven
auto_gratuity_rate.source: Pack-cited from NCL published rate (matches the rate in effect through the entire 2026 batch of NCL packs in this repo)

ship_phone_relay.value: Family handoff card updated 2026-06-11. Pre-edit listed an unverified 'NCL Care Team' direct line; replaced (Prima-precedent fix) with: State Dept Overseas Citizens Services +1-888-407-4747 (toll-free US/Canada) and +1-202-501-4444 (overseas) as the authoritative US-citizens-at-sea path, plus NCL's paid ship-to-shore relay 1-888-627-4477 with explicit verify-before-sailing instruction. Tina Maulsby 910-528-5077 made the primary first-call contact (booking agent of record).
ship_phone_relay.source: https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/international-travel/emergencies.html (State Dept OCS); NCL paid ship-to-shore relay number kept from prior batch with verify-before-sailing instruction
ship_phone_relay.notes: The pre-edit Care Team direct lines (305-436-4000 and 866-234-0292) could not be primary-verified as customer-facing emergency lines (they appear in third-party sources but not on a current NCL.com emergency page in 2026 verification). Replaced with the State Dept path that IS verifiable. Same correction applied to Bliss and Prima packs in this batch.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on any NCL ship. Paid valet laundry + dry cleaning; a 'fill a bag' wash-and-press special (~$30) on most sailings; Latitudes loyalty tiers get discounted/free bags.
laundry_availability.notes: Added 2026-07-06 to meet the current factcheck-gate schema (R7 policies.laundry_availability, which post-dates this sidecar's original 2026-06-11 pass). Fleet-wide NCL policy verified live this session; corroborated by cruisefever.net's self-service-laundry list (NCL absent). Not a headline claim in the pack body.

vancouver_canada_place.value: Embarkation terminal at Canada Place, downtown Vancouver waterfront

lions_gate_bridge.value: Pack pre-edit was WRONG on two counts: said 'a 1,500-foot suspension bridge built 1938' and 'about 60 feet of clearance for Encore at high tide'. Wikipedia (primary source for the bridge): main span is 473 m / 1,552 ft; total span including viaducts is 1,823 m / 5,981 ft; vertical clearance is 61 m / 200 ft; completed 1938. The '60 feet' clearance error was the most serious — 60 ft would not let any large cruise ship pass under, so it was both factually wrong and operationally absurd. Corrected to '1,552-foot main span and about 200 feet of vertical clearance' and added the threading of First Narrows out of Burrard Inlet.

victoria_ogden_point.value: Ogden Point breakwater, ~3 km from downtown; free shuttle or ~$10 round-trip cab/rideshare to Inner Harbour
victoria_ogden_point.source: Pack-cited (Victoria port page); Greater Victoria Harbour Authority public information

san_francisco_pier_27.value: San Francisco Cruise Terminal at Pier 27 on the Embarcadero

los_angeles_world_cruise_center.value: Berths 91-93, World Cruise Center, San Pedro, ~25 miles south of LAX

encore_speedway_track.value: Pack pre-edit said 'three-deck race track at the back' (twice) and 'Encore's go-kart track is the longest at sea'. Primary-source check: The Points Guy + Cruise Critic + Emma Cruises consistently describe Encore Speedway as a TWO-LEVEL track (not three-deck), 1,150 feet long, the longest two-level track at sea when she launched in 2019, extending up to 13 feet over the ship's side. Corrected to 'two-level race track ... 1,150 feet long ... the longest two-level go-kart track at sea when she launched in 2019' — the qualifier matters because newer ships may have surpassed Encore on raw length since 2019.
encore_speedway_track.notes: Also removed the unsourced superlative 'one of the most developed VR centers at sea' applied to Galaxy Pavilion (single-vendor flag from boardroom; held to NCL's neutral published description).

manhattan_room_deck.value: Deck 7 aft (largest of the main dining rooms; dinner-only 5-9 PM)
manhattan_room_deck.notes: Pack pre-edit said 'Deck 7 aft' which matches primary sources.

voice_audit.notes: Final-state voice scan after careful-not-clever pass. 'honest' instances are load-bearing ('honest first-day mistake to avoid', 'practical kit' — the latter dropped from 'honest practical kit' as part of the pass). 'genuinely' single instance is functional ('a balcony genuinely matters' — load-bearing emphasis on the Pacific Coast scenic-cruising case for a balcony). 'iconic' 3 instances all functional (Canada Place white-sail terminal; Empress tea; La Taqueria SF Mission burrito). Zero AI/chatbot/LLM mentions confirmed per operator constraint. NCL Care Team references all replaced with verifiable State Dept OCS + paid relay path. The four templated 'Honest read on X' headers from pre-edit varied to 'What X actually is' / 'Why this overnight is the trip' / 'What the Pacific actually does in mid-October' / unchanged where the construction was unique.
voice_audit.audited_against: voice-audit (2026-06-11 careful-not-clever pass + content boardroom); schema-required fields added 2026-07-06
voice_audit.audit_scope: Full pack body end to end — all sections were read and audited during the original 2026-06-11 careful-not-clever pass. The audit_scope field itself was added 2026-07-06 to satisfy the current voice-audit sidecar schema; it records (does not re-do) that full-body audit.

pnw_october_rain_planning.added_location: Section 4 — Packing List, new subsection 'Planning around the Pacific Northwest October rain'
pnw_october_rain_planning.content_summary: Rainy-day route planning for Vancouver (Canada Place to Gastown under awnings; Aquarium; Museum of Anthropology; Pan Pacific physical connection to terminal) and Victoria (Royal BC Museum, Empress tea, Bay Centre skywalks). Specific gear guidance (waterproof not water-resistant; skip the umbrella). Reframes rainy day as 'plan differently' not 'stay on ship'.
pnw_october_rain_planning.training_data_caution_addressed: Gemini and GPT both flagged that 'drizzly' in the pre-edit pack read as generic cruise weather knowledge rather than specific PNW autumn reality. Addressed.

yvr_flight_cancellation_contingency.added_location: Section 3 — Pre-Cruise Countdown, new subsection 'If your flight to YVR is cancelled or delayed'
yvr_flight_cancellation_contingency.content_summary: Two-day cushion arrival strategy; latest viable YVR arrival time (~noon Monday) backed out from 4:00 PM embark window; SEA-TAC backup route via Quick Shuttle / Amtrak Cascades; immediate-Tina-flag protocol; trip-delay insurance reference.

repositioning_cruise_reality.consensus: Consensus-high (2-of-3 surfaced; both Gemini and GPT flagged 'transition cruise' as a training-data prior risk; Perplexity surfaced as a positive addition)
repositioning_cruise_reality.added_location: Section 9 — Group Rhythm, new subsection 'What \"repositioning cruise\" actually means for the week aboard'
repositioning_cruise_reality.content_summary: Onboard inventory shift mid-week (Alaska → Mexico); crew partly in transition; re-positioning sailings attract long-sea-day cruisers, so Observation Lounge / trivia / library see more use.

solo_after_hours_safety.consensus: Consensus-high (3-of-3 lenses, with Perplexity most concrete)
solo_after_hours_safety.added_location: Section 10 — Solo Cruising, new subsection 'Solo safety — cabin and after-hours'
solo_after_hours_safety.content_summary: Deadbolt + security latch usage; the 'wait for someone' elevator rule; bar exit script for the conversation that goes long; what to do if followed back to deck (Guest Services / casino as 24/7 staffed safe re-entry points); Studio Lounge as quiet re-entry.

first_cruise_day_1_emotional.added_location: Section 11 — First Cruise Notes, new subsection 'The first-time-cruiser Day 1 you weren't warned about'
first_cruise_day_1_emotional.content_summary: The disoriented first three hours (universal); the cabin briefly feeling small then disappearing; the partly-app muster drill timing; the resist-the-pressure-to-book-everything advice; Allison's group gathering as the easy social on-ramp.

attestation_note.added_location: Welcome section, new subsection 'A note on what's first-hand here and what isn't'
attestation_note.content_summary: Explicit declaration that the author hasn't sailed this exact itinerary on this ship; defers to Tina (booking agent, NCL Breakaway Plus class experience) and Allison (on-ship host) where their first-hand knowledge supersedes the pack.

colophon_rows.added_location: Closing section, three new colophon rows above Sources
colophon_rows.content_summary: Last reviewed date (2026-06-11); how facts are checked (primary-source policy + .factcheck.json pointer); accessibility commitment (WCAG 2.1 AA, headings + tables + named links + screen-reader pass).

shorten_first_cruise_section_for_redunda.reason: The First Cruise section is short and additive, not redundant; the boardroom's adopted addition (emotional Day 1) makes the section more load-bearing not less.

condense_two_piece_layer_packing.reason: The PNW climate is exactly the use case where the two-piece layer system pays its keep; condensing it would gut the practical core of the packing list.

shorten_section_5_budget_generic_gratuit.reason: The gratuity numbers reflect this specific 6-night sailing and the Encore-specific rate tiers; the calculator references already short-circuit the math for readers who want detail.

deck_17_18_forward_for_golden_gate_watch.reason: Hallucination — Encore Observation Lounge is on Deck 15 forward (highest passenger lounge with forward views), not Deck 17/18 forward as Perplexity suggested. Pack already correctly directs readers to Deck 15 forward. Not adopted.

longest_two_level_go_kart_track_at_sea_w.value: Encore Speedway, 1,150 feet, two-level, partly enclosed
longest_two_level_go_kart_track_at_sea_w.notes: Qualifier 'when she launched in 2019' added because newer/larger ships post-2019 may have introduced longer tracks; the doctrine bans bare superlatives without temporal grounding.

most_atmospheric_included_room_manhattan.value: Pack soft-superlative kept ('the most atmospheric of the included rooms')
most_atmospheric_included_room_manhattan.source: Cruise Critic + Eat Sleep Cruise + multiple Encore-specific dining reviews (consistently describe Manhattan Room with a dance floor and live music as the standout of the included tier)
most_atmospheric_included_room_manhattan.notes: Soft-superlative within the included-tier on Encore specifically, not a 'best at sea' claim. Within doctrine.

_note_2026_07_06_schema: Brought to the current factcheck-gate schema 2026-07-06: added christening.godparent (mirror of the already-verified Kelly Clarkson godmother), policies.laundry_availability, and the voice-audit schema keys (audited_against/audit_date/audit_scope/risk_rating/cluster_detection.verdict) expressing the existing 2026-06-11 audit. No prior factual claim was changed.
_note_2026_07_06_adversarial_fix: Adversarial audit (2026-07-06): 4 MAJOR + 3 MINOR, all fixed. (1) L268 'three-deck race track' -> two-level Encore Speedway (the sidecar's ship_features already had the correct two-level facts; this surviving body instance is now consistent). (2) L234 Beacon Hill 'the southernmost point of Canada' is FALSE (that's Point Pelee, Ontario) -> corrected to the Mile 0 / Trans-Canada western-terminus marker. (3) L523 'Hudson's' listed as an Encore dining room -> Hudson's is a Prima-class venue; Encore's MDRs are Manhattan Room/Taste/Savor (corrected). (4) Royal BC Museum 'closed Mondays' (2 places) is FALSE -> 'open daily'; the $30 CAD entry softened to ~$18-27. MINOR: SF cable car $8 -> ~$9-12 (fare rose); SF Chinatown 'largest' -> 'oldest' in North America. Sources: thepointsguy Encore Speedway (two-level), en.wikipedia Point Pelee, royalbcmuseum.bc.ca, sfmta.com.
